title: Work Instruction: Sensor Replacement on Conveyor System
purpose: Provide a safe and structured procedure for replacing a defective sensor on a conveyor system.
scope: Applies to maintenance technicians performing sensor replacement on the conveyor system.

safety_precautions.precaution: Disconnect and isolate power before removing covers or working near moving parts.
safety_precautions.reason: Prevents accidental start-up, electrical hazards and injury from moving equipment.

procedure_steps.instruction: Stop the conveyor system and apply lockout/tagout according to site procedure.
procedure_steps.expected_result: The conveyor system is safely isolated.

procedure_steps.instruction: Remove the sensor cover to access the defective sensor.
procedure_steps.expected_result: The sensor is accessible for removal.

procedure_steps.instruction: Disconnect and remove the defective sensor.
procedure_steps.expected_result: The defective sensor has been removed.

procedure_steps.instruction: Install the replacement sensor and adjust its position.
procedure_steps.expected_result: The replacement sensor is mounted and aligned.

procedure_steps.instruction: Verify the sensor signal in the HMI.
procedure_steps.expected_result: The HMI displays the expected sensor signal.

procedure_steps.instruction: Run a test cycle and confirm normal operation.
procedure_steps.expected_result: The conveyor completes the test cycle without faults.
procedure_steps.safety_note: Keep clear of moving parts during the test cycle.

quality_checks.check: Verify sensor signal in the HMI.
quality_checks.acceptance_criteria: The sensor changes state correctly when activated.
The conveyor operates without alarms or unexpected stops.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Incorrect sensor alignment may cause unreliable detection.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Adjust sensor position and verify signal response before returning the system to operation.

missing_information.item: Exact sensor type or part number
missing_information.why_it_matters: The correct replacement part must be verified before performing the procedure.

assumptions: The technician is trained and authorized to perform lockout/tagout.
A compatible replacement sensor is available.
